ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_03301776 represents cola beverage as a category; no exact Coca-Cola branded entry exists in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0LN covers cola-type soft drinks as a category; no Coca-Cola brand-specific code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 represents cola drink as a substance category; closest available match for Coca-Cola regular.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml can of Coca-Cola, equivalent to approximately 330 g, as commonly served in Singapore and convenience outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, carbonated, cola, contains caffeine — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for regular carbonated cola beverage containing caffeine; values are per 100 g (approximately 100 ml) and directly applicable to Coca-Cola regular formulation.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Coca-Cola (Coca-Cola Company, USA)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 63 for regular Coca-Cola is reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2002 International GI table (Foster-Powell et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2002;76:5–56). Glycemic load per 330 ml serving calculated as (63 × 10.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 3.30) / 100 = approximately 22.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (4 mg per 100 g); however, high sugar load may contribute to weight gain and secondary blood pressure elevation with habitual consumption.
hypertension.guidance: Sodium content in Coca-Cola is negligible and does not directly raise blood pressure; however, individuals with hypertension should limit regular cola intake due to its high free-sugar content, which may promote weight gain and indirectly worsen blood pressure control.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free-sugar content (10.6 g per 100 g, entirely as sucrose/HFCS) with a glycemic index of 63 and glycemic load of 22 per 330 ml serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Regular Coca-Cola should be avoided or strictly limited by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, as its high free-sugar content and medium glycemic index (GI 63) produce a high glycemic load per serving (GL ~22), causing rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; sugar-free alternatives are strongly preferred.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free-sugar content linked to elevated triglycerides, hepatic lipogenesis, and increased cardiovascular risk with habitual consumption; zero saturated fat and zero cholesterol.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While Coca-Cola contains no saturated fat or cholesterol, its high free-sugar content (approximately 35 g per 330 ml can) is associated with elevated serum triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise or eliminate regular cola consumption in line with WHO free-sugar guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coca-Cola (Regular), commonly known as Coke or 可乐 (kě lè) in Chinese and 콜라 in Korean, is a globally ubiquitous carbonated soft drink manufactured by The Coca-Cola Company. It is produced by combining carbonated water with sucrose or high-fructose corn syrup, caramel colour (Class IV), phosphoric acid, natural flavours, and caffeine. The beverage is characterised by its distinctive sweet-caramel taste with a sharp, acidic carbonation finish and a dark brown colour. It contains no fat, no protein, and no dietary fibre; its entire caloric content (approximately 41 kcal per 100 ml) derives from free sugars (10.6 g per 100 g). A standard 330 ml can delivers approximately 135 kcal and 35 g of free sugar, exceeding the WHO daily free-sugar recommendation for adults in a single serving. The glycemic index is 63 (medium) with a glycemic load of approximately 22 per can, making it clinically significant for blood glucose management. Sodium content is negligible (4 mg per 100 g). In Singapore, Coca-Cola is widely available across all food service channels including Korean restaurants, hawker centres, fast food outlets, and convenience stores. It is classified as an ultra-processed beverage and is not recommended for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, obesity, or cardiovascular disease. The product is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free, and is generally considered halal.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of Coke?
What is the sugar content of Coca-Cola?
What is the GI of Coke?
Can I drink Coke if I have high blood pressure?
How much sugar is in 330ml of Coke?
What is 可乐 in English?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851) for regular carbonated cola beverage, which is an exact match for Coca-Cola regular formulation. GI value of 63 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and the peer-reviewed 2002 International GI table (Foster-Powell et al.). Both sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero — contains no protein; this is a sugar-water beverage with no protein-containing ingredients. fat_g is confirmed zero — contains no fat; Coca-Cola contains no fat-containing ingredients by formulation. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — carbonated soft drinks contain no dietary fibre. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. All vitamin values (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg) are confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy; Coca-Cola is not fortified with vitamins. glycemic_index is 63 (medium GI, 56–69 range) per University of Sydney GI Database and Foster-Powell et al. 2002. Glycemic load per 330 ml serving = (63 × 10.6 × 3.30) / 100 = 22.0. The food_name in the input JSON was '可乐 Coke'; canonical English name has been set to 'Coca-Cola (Regular)' as the precise product identity. Cuisine tag 'Korean' in the input reflects the restaurant context (Korean restaurant on field survey data), not the origin of the beverage; region_of_origin is correctly set to United States. Korean local name (콜라) is mandatory per G6 Korean cuisine rule applied to the serving context. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301776 and FoodEx2 code AN0LN are closest_parent matches as no brand-specific ontology codes exist for Coca-Cola in these systems. SNOMED CT 226465004 is a closest_parent match.
